nodes.label: AI as coding assistant for data manipulation
nodes.label_negative: AI as unreliable coder requiring heavy oversight
nodes.grounding_span: I use AI to help with quickly writing VBA code for issues I have

nodes.label: AI as iterative collaborator
nodes.grounding_span: I would start with asking a question... Once I get the output of the model I would test it

nodes.label: AI ability to handle complex context
nodes.grounding_span: it would require significant context for what we're looking for in terms of results

nodes.label: AI integration with existing enterprise tools
nodes.grounding_span: these models aren't readily integrated with systems we currently have in place

nodes.label: Organizational cautious adoption of AI
nodes.grounding_span: Our organization's culture has embraced AI and is looking for opportunities to use it to facilitate work, but we recognize that there are limitations

nodes.label: Work efficiency and automation
nodes.grounding_span: automate a significant amount or work

nodes.label: Understanding and control over outputs
nodes.grounding_span: we like to make sure that we understand what is being built and how it was built

nodes.label: Realistic assessment of AI capabilities
nodes.grounding_span: I think we need to be cautious in our implementation of it

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for coding tasks
nodes.grounding_span: One massive success for me was having it help me build an update macro

nodes.label: Frustration with AI on complex problems
nodes.grounding_span: One failure it has was in trying to build a Power BI graph... over 7 hours working on the issue

nodes.label: Skepticism about AI replacing human work
nodes.grounding_span: I think it's overly ambitious to assume that AI can take over significant amounts of my work

nodes.label: Cautious optimism about AI evolution
nodes.grounding_span: I think AI is evolving quickly, but I think we need to be cautious in our implementation of it

nodes.label: Iterative troubleshooting — test, error, refine
nodes.grounding_span: I would start with asking a question... Once I get the output of the model I would test it. 9/10 it fails. I usually send the error message

nodes.label: Pragmatic boundary-setting — knows when to step in
nodes.grounding_span: If I've sent an error message to the AI 2 or 3 times and it starts repeating itself, it's usually a good sign it's missing context or it's time for me to step in
